meaning: adj.像海绵的,柔软,多孔而有弹性的
examples: The ground was soft and spongy.
This spongy tissue serves as a source of calcium for eggshell formation.
You see, adult female birds during egg production produce a layer of spongy bone tissue inside certain long bones.
The fakiness of the humans (they look rather spongy) in the story aside, this cyberspace "cartoon" is remarkable.
But I gazed long and raptly at the wonderful textures of spongy grass, dully reflective flagstones, and dreaming concrete.
